tasks.title: Build task detail view with full editing capabilities
tasks.description: Create a comprehensive task detail view that allows users to view and edit all task fields including title, description, status, priority, tags, scope, evidence, plan, notes, and custom fields. Implement inline editing with proper form controls (pickers for status/priority, tag editor, text editors for multi-line content). This enables complete task lifecycle management without leaving the app.
evidence: No task detail view exists - users must use 'ralph task show <id>' in CLI
No editing capability exists - users must use 'ralph task edit' commands
Task schema has 20+ fields that could be editable
ContentView only has generic CLI command builder, no task-specific UI
plan: Create TaskDetailView SwiftUI view
Design form layout with sections for Basic Info, Scope/Evidence, Plan, Notes, Relationships
Implement status picker with visual indicators (dropdown or segmented control)
Implement priority picker with color coding
Create tag editor with add/remove capability
Add multi-line text editors for description, plan, evidence, notes
Implement scope file picker with drag-and-drop support
Add custom fields editor (key-value pairs)
Create 'Save' button that invokes appropriate ralph task edit commands
Add validation and error handling for edits
Verify by editing a task and confirming changes via 'ralph task show'

tasks.title: Implement sidebar navigation with three-column layout
tasks.description: Replace the current two-tab interface (Quick/Advanced) with a modern three-column sidebar navigation pattern. Left sidebar shows navigation sections (Queue, Task Details, Run, History, Settings). Middle column shows content list (task list, run history). Right column shows detail/inspector view. This matches modern macOS apps like Mail, Notes, and Xcode, providing familiar and efficient navigation.
evidence: Current UI uses TabView with only Quick and Advanced tabs (ContentView.swift:326-332)
Navigation is limited - no way to browse different views simultaneously
Modern macOS apps use NavigationSplitView for multi-column layouts
Current min width 920px doesn't accommodate complex navigation
plan: Replace TabView with NavigationSplitView three-column layout
Create sidebar with sections: Queue, Active Task, Run Control, Analytics, Settings
Implement middle column as content area (task list, command output, etc.)
Implement right column as inspector/detail view
Add sidebar toggle button for collapsible sidebar
Style sidebar with system sidebar background material
Add keyboard shortcuts for navigation (Cmd+1, Cmd+2, etc.)
Implement selection state management between columns
Verify by running app and confirming smooth navigation between sections

tasks.title: Add task creation dialog with template support
tasks.description: Implement a task creation interface with a modal dialog or sheet. Include fields for title, description, priority selection, tag input, and scope selection. Add template support allowing users to pick from predefined templates (bug, feature, refactor, test) that pre-fill task fields. Include a 'Quick Create' mode for rapid task entry and an 'Advanced Create' mode with full field access.
evidence: No task creation UI exists - users must use CLI 'ralph task "..."'
Templates exist in CLI ('ralph task template list') but not exposed in UI
Task creation is a core workflow that should be easily accessible
Advanced tab can run task commands but has no guided workflow
plan: Create TaskCreationView as a modal sheet
Add toolbar button 'New Task' with plus icon
Implement title field with validation
Add description text editor
Create priority picker with visual indicators
Add tag input with autocomplete from existing tags
Create template picker gallery with preview cards
Add scope file picker with drag-and-drop
Implement 'Create' button that runs 'ralph task build' command
Add 'Quick Create' shortcut (Cmd+N) that creates with defaults
Show success feedback and auto-select new task in list
Verify by creating a task and confirming it appears in queue

tasks.title: Build visual task status workflow with drag-and-drop Kanban
tasks.description: Create a Kanban-style board view showing tasks organized by status columns (Draft, Todo, Doing, Done). Allow users to drag tasks between columns to change status. Provide an alternative list view for users who prefer compact displays. Include visual indicators for blocked tasks, tasks with dependencies, and overdue items. This gives users a visual project management experience.
evidence: No visual status management exists - users must use 'ralph task status' CLI commands
Task lifecycle (draft→todo→doing→done) is core to Ralph workflow
Kanban boards are the standard UX pattern for task status management
Queue file stores status but UI provides no visualization
plan: Create KanbanBoardView with horizontal scrolling columns
Implement columns for each status: Draft, Todo, Doing, Done, Rejected
Create task cards showing title, priority badge, tags
Implement drag-and-drop using SwiftUI dropDestination API
Add column headers with task count badges
Implement status change via drag (triggers 'ralph task status' command)
Add visual indicators for blocked tasks (dependency warnings)
Include 'Start Work' button that sets status to Doing + sets started_at
Add toggle between Kanban view and List view
Verify by dragging a task to different columns and checking status updates

tasks.title: Implement task dependency visualization with graph view
tasks.description: Create a visual dependency graph showing task relationships (depends_on, blocks, relates_to). Use a force-directed graph or hierarchical tree layout to display how tasks connect. Include visual indicators for circular dependencies, critical path highlighting, and blocking relationships. This helps users understand task sequencing and identify blockers at a glance.
evidence: CLI has 'ralph queue graph' command but no visual output in UI
Dependencies are critical to Ralph workflow but invisible in current app
Users cannot see why tasks are blocked without running CLI commands
Graph visualization is a high-value feature for project management
plan: Create DependencyGraphView using SwiftUI Canvas or SpriteKit
Implement node representation for tasks with status colors
Draw edges for depends_on (arrow), blocks (red), relates_to (dashed)
Add force-directed layout algorithm for node positioning
Implement zoom and pan gestures for large graphs
Highlight critical path (longest dependency chain)
Add warning indicators for circular dependencies
Implement node selection to show task details
Add 'ralph queue graph --format dot' integration for export
Include alternative tree view for hierarchical visualization
Verify by creating tasks with dependencies and confirming graph renders correctly

tasks.title: Add real-time queue file watching with automatic UI updates
tasks.description: Implement filesystem watching for .ralph/queue.json and .ralph/done.json so the UI updates automatically when tasks are modified externally (via CLI or another process). Use FSEvents API to detect changes and refresh the task list without manual reload. Add subtle animation for new tasks, status changes, and removals to provide visual feedback.
evidence: Current app requires manual 'Reload CLI Spec' button press for updates
No file watching exists - UI can become stale if queue changes externally
CLI commands modify queue.json but app doesn't know about changes
Real-time sync is expected behavior for modern macOS apps
plan: Add FSEvents-based file watcher for queue.json and done.json
Implement debouncing to avoid excessive reloads (500ms delay)
Add auto-refresh of task list when file changes detected
Implement subtle animations for new tasks (slide in)
Add status change animations (color transition)
Show 'Queue updated' toast notification on external changes
Handle file conflicts gracefully (merge vs overwrite)
Add manual refresh button as fallback
Verify by running 'ralph task' in terminal and confirming UI updates automatically

tasks.title: Create run control panel for task execution with live output
tasks.description: Build a dedicated run control panel that shows the currently running task (if any), execution progress, and live console output. Include buttons to start/stop execution, run the next task, or run a continuous loop. Show runner status, phase information, and estimated completion time. This provides a cockpit for managing the AI agent execution.
evidence: Current app has basic 'Run' button but no execution control panel
No live progress indication during task execution
Console output exists but is plain text without syntax highlighting
No way to see which task is running or execution phase
plan: Create RunControlView with execution status dashboard
Show current task card with title, ID, status
Add progress indicator showing current phase (1/3, 2/3, 3/3)
Implement live console with ANSI color code parsing
Add 'Run Next' button for executing ralph run one
Add 'Start Loop' / 'Stop Loop' toggle for continuous execution
Show runner configuration (model, phases) for current run
Add elapsed time timer and estimated completion
Implement 'Stop After Current' graceful stop button
Add execution history log with success/failure indicators
Verify by running a task and confirming live updates appear

tasks.title: Build analytics dashboard with productivity metrics and charts
tasks.description: Create an analytics dashboard showing productivity metrics including task completion rate, velocity (tasks per day), current streak, tag breakdown, and task aging. Use SwiftUI Charts for visualizations. Include burndown chart, completion history timeline, and priority distribution pie chart. This gives users insights into their work patterns.
evidence: CLI has 'ralph productivity' and 'ralph queue stats' commands
CLI has 'ralph queue burndown' and 'ralph queue history'
No visual analytics exist in macOS app
Charts.framework is available in macOS 13+ for rich visualizations
plan: Create AnalyticsDashboardView with Chart components
Implement burndown chart showing remaining tasks over time
Add velocity chart (tasks completed per day/week)
Create streak indicator with current and longest streak
Add tag breakdown pie/donut chart
Implement priority distribution bar chart
Add task aging heatmap (warning/stale/rotten buckets)
Show completion rate percentage with trend indicator
Add time range picker (7d, 30d, 90d, all time)
Integrate 'ralph productivity summary' command for data
Verify by comparing chart data to CLI output

tasks.title: Add menu bar extra for quick task access and status
tasks.description: Implement a menu bar extra (status bar item) that provides quick access to Ralph without opening the full app. Show current task status, next task title, and quick actions (run next, add quick task, open full app). This enables lightweight interaction for users who want to monitor and control Ralph from anywhere.
evidence: No menu bar integration exists - app must be open to see status
Menu bar extras are standard for background/utility apps on macOS
Users may want quick status without full app window
Menu bar provides always-available access
plan: Create MenuBarExtra using App protocol or NSStatusBar
Add Ralph icon to menu bar with status indicator
Show current/next task title in menu
Add 'Run Next Task' menu item with keyboard shortcut
Add 'Quick Add Task' menu item that opens input dialog
Show task counts by status (Todo: 5, Doing: 1, Done: 12)
Add 'Open Full App' menu item
Implement preferences toggle to enable/disable menu bar extra
Show notifications for task completion via menu bar
Add recent tasks submenu for quick access
Verify by clicking menu bar icon and testing all menu items

tasks.title: Implement global keyboard shortcuts and command palette
tasks.description: Add comprehensive keyboard shortcuts for power users including Cmd+N (new task), Cmd+R (run next), Cmd+K (command palette), Cmd+1/2/3 (switch views). Implement a command palette (Cmd+Shift+P or Cmd+K) that provides quick access to all app actions with fuzzy search. This enables efficient keyboard-driven workflows.
evidence: No keyboard shortcuts exist beyond system defaults
Power users expect keyboard-driven workflows
Command palettes are standard in modern dev tools (VS Code, Raycast)
Shortcuts significantly improve productivity for frequent actions
plan: Define keyboard shortcut scheme (Cmd+N new, Cmd+R run, Cmd+F find, Cmd+K palette)
Implement keyboard shortcut handlers in window
Create CommandPaletteView with fuzzy search
Register all app actions in palette with descriptions
Add shortcut indicators in menu items and tooltips
Implement view switching shortcuts (Cmd+1 Queue, Cmd+2 Run, Cmd+3 Analytics)
Add escape key handling to dismiss modals/palette
Implement space key to preview selected task
Add shortcuts for status changes (Cmd+Shift+S for status menu)
Document all shortcuts in Help menu
Verify by testing each shortcut and confirming action fires
